title: Wishlist Pulse
objective: Memorize each incoming pitch pattern, replay it on a 3-lane touch pad, and bank as many wishlists as possible before you lose 3 hearts to bad pitches or hesitation.

controls.input: Tap the left, center, or right pad
controls.outcome: Inputs the matching beat in the shown sequence

controls.input: Press and hold a glowing pad until the ring closes
controls.outcome: Completes sustained notes for bonus accuracy

controls.input: When a red feedback beat appears, tap the next two beats in reverse order
controls.outcome: Turns harsh feedback into a combo extender instead of a penalty

steps: A crowd meter fills and a short synth pattern plays across three dev booth pads: Code, Art, and Pitch.
The pattern repeats once visually with bright pulse trails and audio cues.
You replay it from memory on touch controls.
Every few rounds, a red Feedback beat appears; from that point, the next two beats must be entered in reverse order.
Correct replays earn wishlists, raise the tempo, and lengthen the pattern.
A mistake, missed hold, or timeout costs a heart; lose 3 hearts and the run ends in about 2-4 minutes.
scoring: Each correct beat gives points; finishing a full pattern grants wishlists. Perfect patterns build a Hype combo multiplier up to x8, and successfully handling a Feedback reversal adds a burst bonus. Leaderboards rank best
tips: Listen first; each lane has a distinct tone so the melody carries the order.
Treat red Feedback beats as a pivot point: prepare to reverse the next two taps.
Short safe streaks keep your multiplier alive better than panicked recovery taps.
Use holds to steady your rhythm; they are worth more and help you reset timing.

trendContext.title: Indie devs: Is it really that hard to get Steam wishlists ? Honest experiences
